briefdescription.para: \par \b Purpose\/ \: To extract EXIF metadata from images (JPG, TIFF, etc.) and merge it with base file metadata — decodes fields like camera make/model, dates, resolution, and EXIF version.

detaileddescription.para: \par \b Scope\/ \: Image EXIF metadata extraction: camera info, date/time, resolution, orientation, user comments. \par \b Input\/ \: file_path via extract(), config with recognized image formats. \par \b Output\/ \: dict merging BaseMetadataExtractor fields + EXIF-derived fields (date_time, make, model, exif_version, orientation, etc.). \par \b Links\/ \: [USES_API(8): BaseMetadataExtractor, PIL.ExifTags, PIL.Image, piexif.load] \par \b Invariants\/ \:

listitem.para: _get_exif NEVER raises — on any error returns {"broken_image": True}.
All EXIF values are encoded/parsed through type-specific helper methods (__encode_exif, __parse_int, __parse_date, __parse_float). \par \b Rationale\/ \:

detaileddescription.para: Q: Why use piexif.load instead of PIL's built-in _getexif()? A: piexif provides a standardized EXIF dictionary format that is easier to map to specific keys, and handles vendor-specific EXIF quirks better. \par \b Changes\/ \:
LAST_CHANGE: [v1.0.0 — Semantic template markup and LDD logging added.] \par \b Module Map\/ \:
CLASS 8[Image EXIF metadata extractor] => ImageMetadataExtractor \par \b Use Cases\/ \:
